subject: grammar
title: Emphasis: Cleft Sentences
instruction: Rewrite the sentences below using it- or what-clauses to emphasize the underlined words (provided in the prompt).
passage:
Example: I find writing essays really difficult. 
Answer: What I find really difficult is writing essays.

questions.text: The government needs a lot more loyal supporters. (Emphasize: a lot more loyal supporters)
questions.answer: What the government needs is a lot more loyal supporters.

questions.text: We do not want words. We want action. (Emphasize: action)
questions.answer: We do not want words. What we want is action.

questions.text: I really like reading novels. I do not enjoy watching TV. (Emphasize: watching TV)
questions.answer: I really like reading novels. What I do not enjoy is watching TV.

questions.text: In my country the bride is the most important person at a wedding. (Emphasize: the bride)
questions.answer: In my country it is the bride who is the most important person at a wedding.

questions.text: Governments should be dealing with the causes of poverty, not the results of it. (Emphasize: the causes of poverty)
questions.answer: It is the causes of poverty that governments should be dealing with, not the results of it.

questions.text: I first decided to study medicine when I was ill in hospital as a child. (Emphasize: when I was ill in hospital as a child)
questions.answer: It was when I was ill in hospital as a child that I first decided to study medicine.

questions.text: When you are seriously ill your family suffer the most. (Emphasize: your family)
questions.answer: When you are seriously ill it is your family that suffer the most.
